encoder.c(3) Library Functions Manual encoder.c(3)

NAME

encoder.c - FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct h265_encoder

Typedefs


typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Functions


h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)
void h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t *ascii_data, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_request_keyframe (h265_encoder_t *encoder)
asciichat_error_t h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint8_t *output_buf, size_t *output_size)
void h265_encoder_get_stats (h265_encoder_t *encoder, uint64_t *total_frames, uint64_t *keyframes, uint32_t *avg_bitrate)

Detailed Description

FFmpeg HEVC encoder for ASCII art frames.

Definition in file encoder.c.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct h265_encoder h265_encoder_t

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t h265_encode (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint16_t width, uint16_t height, const uint8_t * ascii_data, uint8_t * output_buf, size_t * output_size)

Definition at line 252 of file encoder.c.

253                                                                         {
254   if (!encoder || !ascii_data || !output_buf || !output_size) {
255     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder arguments");
256   }
257 
258   if (*output_size < 5) {
259     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
260   }
261 
262   asciichat_error_t result = h265_encoder_reconfigure(encoder, width, height);
263   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
264     return result;
265   }
266 
267   h265_encoder_ascii_to_yuv420(ascii_data, width, height, encoder->yuv_buf);
268 
269   // Setup frame data pointers
270   encoder->frame->data[0] = encoder->yuv_buf;
271   encoder->frame->data[1] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height;
272   encoder->frame->data[2] = encoder->yuv_buf + width * height + (width / 2) * (height / 2);
273   encoder->frame->linesize[0] = width;
274   encoder->frame->linesize[1] = width / 2;
275   encoder->frame->linesize[2] = width / 2;
276   encoder->frame->width = width;
277   encoder->frame->height = height;
278   encoder->frame->format = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
279 
280   // Request keyframe if needed
281   if (encoder->request_keyframe) {
282     encoder->frame->pict_type = AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I;
283     encoder->request_keyframe = false;
284   }
285 
286   // Encode frame
287   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Sending frame to encoder: %ux%u format=%d", width, height, encoder->frame->format);
288   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->frame);
289   if (send_ret < 0) {
290     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_send_frame failed: %d", send_ret);
291     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to send frame to encoder");
292   }
293 
294   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Receiving encoded packet...");
295   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
296   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
297     // EAGAIN is normal - encoder needs more frames before outputting a packet
298     log_dev("H265_ENCODE: EAGAIN - encoder buffering frames (normal), waiting for next frame");
299     *output_size = 0; // No output this time
300     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
301   }
302   if (recv_ret < 0) {
303     log_error("H265_ENCODE: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d (EAGAIN=%d)", recv_ret, AVERROR(EAGAIN));
304     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive encoded packet");
305   }
306   log_debug("H265_ENCODE: Got packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
307 
308   uint8_t flags = 0;
309   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
310     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
311     encoder->keyframes++;
312   }
313 
314   if (width != encoder->current_width || height != encoder->current_height) {
315     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_SIZE_CHANGE;
316   }
317 
318   size_t header_size = 5;
319   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
320   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
321 
322   if (required_size > *output_size) {
323     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
324   }
325 
326   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
327   output_buf[0] = flags;
328   output_buf[1] = (width >> 8) & 0xFF;
329   output_buf[2] = width & 0xFF;
330   output_buf[3] = (height >> 8) & 0xFF;
331   output_buf[4] = height & 0xFF;
332 
333   // Write encoded data
334   if (packet_size > 0) {
335     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
336   }
337 
338   *output_size = required_size;
339   encoder->total_frames++;
340 
341   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
342 }




References h265_encoder::codec_ctx,
    h265_encoder::current_height, h265_encoder::current_width,
    h265_encoder::frame, h265_encoder::keyframes,
    h265_encoder::packet, h265_encoder::request_keyframe,
    h265_encoder::total_frames, and h265_encoder::yuv_buf.

    h265_client_capture_and_encode().






h265_encoder_t * h265_encoder_create (uint16_t
  initial_width, uint16_t initial_height)


Definition at line 39 of file encoder.c.


39                                                                                      {
40   if (initial_width == 0 || initial_height == 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Frame dimensions must be non-zero");
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   h265_encoder_t *enc = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(h265_encoder_t), h265_encoder_t *);
46   if (!enc) {
47     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate encoder structure");
48     return NULL;
49   }
50 
51   // Find best available HEVC encoder (hevc_vaapi, hevc_nvenc, libx265, or software fallback)
52   const AVCodec *codec = avcodec_find_encoder(AV_CODEC_ID_HEVC);
53   if (!codec) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "HEVC encoder not found");
55     SAFE_FREE(enc);
56     return NULL;
57   }
58 
59   enc->codec_ctx = avcodec_alloc_context3(codec);
60   if (!enc->codec_ctx) {
61     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate codec context");
62     SAFE_FREE(enc);
63     return NULL;
64   }
65 
66   // Configure codec context
67   enc->codec_ctx->width = initial_width;
68   enc->codec_ctx->height = initial_height;
69   enc->codec_ctx->pix_fmt = AV_PIX_FMT_YUV420P;
70   enc->codec_ctx->time_base = (AVRational){1, 30};
71   enc->codec_ctx->framerate = (AVRational){30, 1};
72 
73   // Set ultrafast preset for low-latency encoding
74   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "preset", "ultrafast", 0) < 0) {
75     log_warn("Failed to set preset to ultrafast, using default");
76   }
77   if (av_opt_set(enc->codec_ctx->priv_data, "tune", "zerolatency", 0) < 0) {
78     log_warn("Failed to set tune to zerolatency, using default");
79   }
80 
81   // Open codec (capture x265 library output during initialization)
82   int codec_open_result = 0;
83   LOG_IO("hevc", {
84       codec_open_result = avcodec_open2(enc->codec_ctx, codec, NULL);
85   });
86 
87   if (codec_open_result < 0) {
88     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, "Failed to open HEVC encoder");
89     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
90     SAFE_FREE(enc);
91     return NULL;
92   }
93 
94   // Allocate frame and packet
95   enc->frame = av_frame_alloc();
96   enc->packet = av_packet_alloc();
97 
98   if (!enc->frame || !enc->packet) {
99     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate frame/packet");
100     if (enc->frame)
101       av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
102     if (enc->packet)
103       av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
104     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
105     SAFE_FREE(enc);
106     return NULL;
107   }
108 
109   enc->current_width = initial_width;
110   enc->current_height = initial_height;
111 
112   // Allocate YUV420P buffer (Y plane + U/V planes)
113   size_t stride = initial_width;
114   enc->yuv_buf_size = stride * initial_height * 3 / 2;
115   enc->yuv_buf = SAFE_MALLOC(enc->yuv_buf_size, uint8_t *);
116   if (!enc->yuv_buf) {
117     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate YUV buffer");
118     av_frame_free(&enc->frame);
119     av_packet_free(&enc->packet);
120     avcodec_free_context(&enc->codec_ctx);
121     SAFE_FREE(enc);
122     return NULL;
123   }
124 
125   log_info("FFmpeg HEVC encoder created: %ux%u (codec: %s)", initial_width, initial_height, codec->name);
126 
127   // Register encoder for debugging
128   NAMED_REGISTER_H265_ENCODER(enc, "h265_encoder", NULL);
129 
130   return enc;
131 }

    h265_encoder::current_height, h265_encoder::current_width,
    h265_encoder::frame, h265_encoder::packet,
    h265_encoder::yuv_buf, and h265_encoder::yuv_buf_size.

void
  h265_encoder_destroy (h265_encoder_t * encoder)


Definition at line 133 of file encoder.c.


133                                                    {
134   if (!encoder)
135     return;
136 
137   if (encoder->frame) {
138     av_frame_free(&encoder->frame);
139   }
140   if (encoder->packet) {
141     av_packet_free(&encoder->packet);
142   }
143   if (encoder->codec_ctx) {
144     // Capture x265 encoder cleanup output (final frame stats, etc.)
145     LOG_IO("hevc", {
146       avcodec_free_context(&encoder->codec_ctx);
147     });
148   }
149   if (encoder->yuv_buf) {
150     SAFE_FREE(encoder->yuv_buf);
151   }
152   SAFE_FREE(encoder);
153 }

    h265_encoder::frame, h265_encoder::packet, and
    h265_encoder::yuv_buf.

asciichat_error_t
  h265_encoder_flush (h265_encoder_t * encoder, uint8_t * output_buf,
  size_t * output_size)


Definition at line 350 of file encoder.c.


350                                                                                                         {
351   if (!encoder || !output_buf || !output_size) {
352     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Invalid encoder or output parameters for flush");
353   }
354 
355   if (*output_size < 5) {
356     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small (minimum 5 bytes)");
357   }
358 
359   // Send NULL frame to signal end of stream (flushes encoder)
360   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Sending NULL frame to encoder to flush buffered data");
361   int send_ret = avcodec_send_frame(encoder->codec_ctx, NULL);
362   if (send_ret < 0) {
363     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_send_frame(NULL) failed: %d", send_ret);
364     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to flush encoder");
365   }
366 
367   // Try to receive a packet
368   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Attempting to receive flushed packet...");
369   int recv_ret = avcodec_receive_packet(encoder->codec_ctx, encoder->packet);
370   if (recv_ret == AVERROR(EAGAIN)) {
371     log_dev("H265_FLUSH: EAGAIN - no more frames available");
372     *output_size = 0;
373     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
374   }
375   if (recv_ret == AVERROR_EOF) {
376     log_debug("H265_FLUSH: EOF - encoder is flushed");
377     *output_size = 0;
378     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
379   }
380   if (recv_ret < 0) {
381     log_error("H265_FLUSH: avcodec_receive_packet failed: %d", recv_ret);
382     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEDIA_DECODE, "Failed to receive flushed packet");
383   }
384 
385   log_debug("H265_FLUSH: Got flushed packet: size=%d", (int)encoder->packet->size);
386 
387   // Check if packet size exceeds output buffer
388   size_t header_size = 5;
389   size_t packet_size = encoder->packet->size;
390   size_t required_size = header_size + packet_size;
391 
392   if (required_size > *output_size) {
393     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_SIZE, "Output buffer too small: need %zu, have %zu", required_size, *output_size);
394   }
395 
396   // Write header: [flags][width:u16][height:u16]
397   uint8_t flags = 0;
398   if (encoder->frame->pict_type == AV_PICTURE_TYPE_I) {
399     flags |= H265_ENCODER_FLAG_KEYFRAME;
400     encoder->keyframes++;
401   }
402 
403   output_buf[0] = flags;
404   output_buf[1] = (encoder->current_width >> 8) & 0xFF;
405   output_buf[2] = encoder->current_width & 0xFF;
406   output_buf[3] = (encoder->current_height >> 8) & 0xFF;
407   output_buf[4] = encoder->current_height & 0xFF;
408 
409   // Write encoded data
410   if (packet_size > 0) {
411     memcpy(output_buf + header_size, encoder->packet->data, packet_size);
412   }
413 
414   *output_size = required_size;
415   encoder->total_frames++;
416 
417   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
418 }
